post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 195: Trump Mafia!
Flavor Source: Trump webtoon
Game Mechanics: Closed Non-BM Game
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for rules

Moderators
I, Charge, will be the (honest) moderator, and xzhang will be the co-moderator. All non-flavor posts I make shall be in blue, all town-flavored posts in green, all mafia-flavored posts, if any, in red, and all third-party flavored posts, if any, in violet.

Edicts
- Follow all rules, including this one, in this game and in the mafia forum.
- If you are a spectator or you are dead, you may not post. If it is night, you may not post. If you are a player, you may not post/communicate about this game or anything directly identifiable as content in this game anywhere outside this thread, unless specifically allowed permission by the moderator.
- Play by your win condition. Game-throwing is strictly prohibited.
- Do not conceal text using any method or use moderator colors.
- Do not add tags.
- Do not edit or delete your post. If necessary, use EBWOP or EBWON.
- Do not quote Role PM’s, or pretend to quote Role PM’s.
*Roleclaiming is allowed, but flavorclaiming is strictly prohibited; details in Role PMs

Laws Regarding Votes
- Vote in the format Vote: [insert player name here]. Votes must follow this exact format; they will not be counted if the player name is punctuated, capitalized, spaced, or spelled incorrectly. The following is a correct vote: Vote: e_is_cool. The following are examples of incorrect votes: Vote: e_is_cooI, Vote: e_is _cool, Vote: E_is_cool, vote: e_is_cool, Vote:e_is_cool, Vote: e_is_cool. Votes in quotes or hide tags will not be counted.
- Vote for no hang by posting Vote: No Hang. Unvote by posting Unvote.
- Votes will be reset at the start of each new game day.
- Official Votecounts will be made at the discretion of the moderator, but players may request votecounts using !votecount
- If a Votecount is incorrect, and the moderator or co-moderator, if any, receives no notice within 24 hours, then the Votecount shall become official.
- The game is majority hang, meaning that the player (including “No Hang”) with the majority of the votes in the game will be hanged at the very point the majority vote is reached. Thereafter, Twilight will occur, until the End of Day notification is posted. Players may continue to post in Twilight, but no votes will be counted.
- Once the end of day votecount is posted, and a player, if any, is announced dead, the player is officially dead, and Night immediately occurs.
- There is a confirm rule, which players should address as soon as possible. The directions are found somewhere within these rules.

Laws Regarding Activity
- Each Day shall be 336 hours long. The moderator may extend Day by any time for whatever reason.
- The end of the Day shall occur every time 336 hours of consecutive Day time has passed.
- Each Night shall be 72 hours long. During Night, players with night actions may perform their night action by posting in their Role PM. Any requests posted during Day will not be processed. At the end of night, players who have any night actions will lose their ability to perform a night action, if they have any.
- Once the End of Night notice is posted, the next Day will officially start.
- You must show that you understand these edicts and laws by posting in your Role PM the two words I spell with the first letter of the titles of almost every hide tag in this post. If you do not, your votes will not be counted.
- Players must post a non-prod-dodge-post at least once every 24 hours, if not on V/LA. Once the first 24 hours have gone by, if the player has not posted according to this rule, the player will receive an in-game prod. Once the next 24 hours have gone by, the player will receive a PM prod. The player will then accumulate 1 PM prod per 24 hours after the first. After 72 hours, the moderator will replace the player, unless the player posts before the moderator finds a replacement. The moderator will also replace the player if the player has received a total of 5 or more PM prods.
- Request V/LA by posting V/LA: [insert date here] to [insert date here] in the thread or Role PM. As a general rule of thumb, do not post V/LA’s longer than five days.
- Request replacement by posting Request Replacement in the thread or Role PM.

Your Behavior
- Do not behave inappropriately. This includes language and attitude.
- The player receives a warning for each minor rule infraction. A newbie may receive up to 2 warnings, and an SE/IC up to 1. If a player commits any rule infraction after they have run out of warnings, or if they commit a major rule infraction, they will be modkilled (or force-replaced).

ReadMe.exe
EoN0
Melly Day occurs on February 29th each year. It’s the day that Melly killed Certa, and also the day she killed herself. It’s told in a legend that Melly appears every February 29th looking for Certa…

Charge, an ordinary citizen
You are an ordinary citizen (Vanilla Townie) in Roserocarta, the world of God. You were not particularly close to the crime scene, but it seems that Yeohadan sees you as a suspect involved in the possible crime.

Your only power, other than your inborn power, is your voice and your vote.
You are Town-Aligned.
residing in the Gods’ world, died coincidentally on 2/29/12. Xzhang was discovered dead this date 4 years ago. Their deaths seem similar to the death of Senika 16 years ago, which has been unsolved all these years. Hopefully, the stage that the Creator set up will be able to find the murderer, if any, and avenge the victims’ death.

EoD1
On the first day, the citizens noticed that one particular person seemed too obviously suspicious. hannahptl (Certa, Jester) had been lurking around and claiming that she was the one who killed Melly. The citizens, finding the claim believable, hanged her.

EoN1
A few hours after the sun set, a few stealthy figures slipped by in the darkness. Other than the rustling noises they accidentally produced while moving, the night was calm and peaceful...

In the wee hours of night, Sena, who had been meeting with Ten, her brother, at Yeohadan to discuss their work on the latest case (and complaining about overtime), stretched and yawned and finally left the organization. She couldn't wait to get a good nights sleep to refresh and refocus on the Melly cases tomorrow.

At the same time, Hiasen had been pondering about Certa, and whether he should have killed him. He tried to reason that Certa would have killed his father, one way or another, if he had not died. Yet Hiasen could not suppress the guilt and shame rising up in his heart - he had thought of Certa as a kind of foster father and friend. He sighed, deciding to rest his mind.

The morning sun rose, its rays of sunlight shining upon the God's world. Suddenly, a startling scream broke the morning silence. Someone had discovered a body in an abandoned laboratory. Upon inspection, Yeohadan police officers concluded that fishy15 (Sena, Mason) was dead. Just as they were about to examine the body, however, they received another notice. The Yeohadan officers covered the eyes of their colleague, and turned to address the new case: another one of the suspects behind the Melly Day cases, HannahW (Hiasen, Cop), was found dead. Her corpse had been found under the shadows of an unnoticing little corner in the streets. It was strange that a child such as him was out so late at night, the Yeohadan officers muttered amongst each other.

EoD2
Under the custody of the Yeohadan officers, the suspects had nothing much to do. Much of the day passed by silently, although a few brawls broke out. The officers yawned, seeing nothing of interest. As the last police left his shift, however, he noticed that tikkitamatoa was getting strangled by many of the other suspects. He shouted at them, and they froze in silence.

"What an unruly bunch," the last officer thought. He would have to bring this up with his superiors tomorrow. Hopefully, they'd be able to separate the citizens and criminals under custody so that they wouldn't have to deal with such egregrious behavior.

No one died.

EoN2
It was finally morning. The people of Roserocarta yawned and trickled out of bed, as the sun shone brightly in the sky. The citizens dressed, took their morning breakfast - some going to a nearby cafe. Then, they took to their daily business: whatever they did on the weekends.

An officer groaned. He was supposed to go camping with his family today, but the previous man on duty - a sloth, thought the officer - had had some "emergency" and thus left the shift open. His superiors had determined that a family vacation on a nice Sunday morning was not worth the important duty of guarding a couple of suspects.

He shook his head at this thought, and by chance saw the time on his watch. He glanced up again. The line in front of him was too long. He'd never get the coffee he needed and get to work on time.

"What bad luck," he said, reluctantly exiting the shop. He decided to order his coffee at work. It would be a bit late, and it probably wouldn't be fresh, but it would have to do. Thinking this, the officer sighed as he hustled himself to Yeohadan.

It was a strangely beautiful morning, and the sky was especially clear even in front of the large looming building in front of the officer. He thought of what his wife and children would be doing. His superiors had better give him a day off for today, he thought, as he pushed the door open. He rummaged through a few papers at the front desk, looking for the rooms he was to guard today. It was such boring duty; he'd rather take case notes on the recent villains in the Wanted papers.

He took the keys to the room, and whistled as he walked down the long hallway. He had ordered some coffee from the front desk, and it would arrive in a few minutes.

He thought he smelled something for a second. Perhaps it was coffee? But it couldn't have arrived so soon...
A few steps further he noticed the pungent smell of blood. Alarmed, the officer ran down to the room he was to guard and peered in.

There were two corpses sitting among the fourteen - no, twelve - people in the room. NDMath (Vanilla Townie) and tikkitamatoa (Vanilla Townie) had died through the night... yet the other people in the room were sleeping peacefully. The officer quickly called his superior.

"Are the people I am guarding a group of murderers?" He asked. There was a silence.

"What happened?" His boss questioned from the other side. "They are suspects to a certain case but they haven't been judged yet," the boss added, figuring that the content was appropriate for his employee to know.

"There are two dead people," answered the officer.

EoD3
The superiors of the officer ignored when he called. They had suspects, but they were citizens, and surely had rational thought processes. Yet this day proved them wrong. There lie kevinmathz (Ran, Miller) in a pool of blood at the 11 other suspects' feet. They'd killed him, ignoring his claims of innocence throughout the game.

Another one down...

But the police couldn't comprehend. The criminal(s) was narrowing down the list of suspects... which would lead the police to find them. Why would they do so?

EoN3
He whisked on his white lab coat, peeked into the dim moonlight, and after seeing no one, he cautiously slid into the shadows. He would report to them what he knew thusfar of the investigation.

Silver light speckled onto the long billowy tails of his coat as he exited into the streets. It was inconvenient to don the white robe - it was an eyecatcher in the darkness of the evening, but he wore it nonetheless, since he knew he was most recognizable doing so. It would ensure that he wasn't wrongly attacked by his allies.

And then, in the streets, he let out a barely audible gasp - he felt a smooth knife rip the side of his coat and slide into his stomach. Immediately, he clutched his front side to stop the blood from leaking.

"Who is it?" Thornclaw9000 (Psychi, Traitor) whipped around as he fell towards the cement. The last he saw was an ominous smile hidden behind the tails of his white lab coat... or was it the culprit's?

EoD4
They were locked in the dungeon, and had nothing much better to do than point fingers at one another. The sooner the culprit(s) was found, the sooner they would all be released.

But of course, the culprit(s) didn't want to be found.

In the midst of the chaos, the prisoners became restless. Yet, only a day later, they were snoozing as light shone through the small windows in the room. They'd used up all their energy in arguments, important or trivial.

Bored, one prisoner decided to claim that they were part of the Yeohadan police. The other prisoners stared at him. Why would he be in prison if he were the police? Then, another prisoner woke up to the shouting, and realizing the news, decided to announce the news that he was an actual member of Yeohadan, and that he had never known of any person like the first prisoner who had claimed. The first stared in shock, stating that he had done it merely as a joke, because clearly everyone was very bored.

As the day neared its end, the suspects found their energy draining. They'd gotten tired of the place. There was no way they would find the culprit(s) by just sitting and pointing fingers.

In particular, everyone seemed to suspect one particular person. Some shoved said person against the wall, while others tried to question him, or torture him with whatever tools were available in the room.

After enough of this ensued, a couple of people voiced that it seemed that this one person was innocent - they'd been bruised pretty badly, and seemed to stick to their words.

"Stop!" They called, but it was too late. Kbwuf (Vanilla Townie) slumped down onto the ground, as his spirit finally left his body after undergoing grueling questioning by the mishandling of the other suspects.

EoN4
There she sat, stirring in the dark room. Her eyes cautiously swiveled around, and she stood up when she noticed that the figures in the darkness had no signs of moving. She stretched, and looked around, scouting for a path leading outside the room. Or perhaps, she'd have to use his power. She sighed, and slipped out the room, and then out onto the sidewalk in front of the Yeohadan building.

She needed a fresh breath of air after nights staying locked up in the room - nights with those corpses staring up at him. She never thought she would have to dirty her own hands, but it had been necessary.

As she stood outside in the cool night air, she heard footsteps approaching behind her.
It was probably him.

She turned around to face the figure.

"You?" She asked in confusion as she saw someone she didn't expect. As she focused his power in an effort to kill this person, she realized she was a second too late - and soon she saw himself shedding blood on the dark sidewalk. There lie bigmath (Eftichia Leader, Mafia Goon) in a pool of murky red ink.

Meanwhile, a certain someone had been strolling around the Mana Association. He hadn't heard of news from Yeohadan for ages, and he was quite worried about Hiasen. He'd tried to obtain info and asked to visit, but the Yeohadan soldiers had been adamant that the official stay out. He could have entered by force, but he'd then enrage Yeohadan, which probably wouldn't benefit Hiasen.

Perhaps he could ask Psychi to help, he thought, and he visited the Mana Association leader's room. He was met with a silent and empty room. Classic.

Just as he sighed and turned to leave, he was met with a face... a familiar one.
"Why are you- " he asked, before he was shot across the room by a blast of power.

He scrambled to collect his thoughts, facing his opponent. Why would that person be here, he wondered. His thoughts were cut short as he was sent propelling against the corner of Psychi's desk. Finally reacting, he tried to grasp the air around him to choke his opponent. However, he himself was weak, and he begin becoming dizzy as he saw the figure approach him, seemingly cool.

Of course, his powers wouldn't have an effect on her, thought chess24 (Kasin, Doctor) as he left the realm of consciousness.

EoD5
The suspects who were alive sat around in a circle, discussing the best plan to find out who was the criminal. They figured the faster they did that, the faster the rest of them would be set free - if they wouldn't get murdered by the criminal. Fed up with the little progress, one particular person decided to claim that had been awake during the Night to identify that another person hadn't moved anywhere. Another person chimed in, naming that someone, to the surprise of all the suspects in the circle. They started to wonder if these two people were in on a plan.

Despite his confession that he hadn't been awake during the Night, and his cries of innocence, BobDBuilder321 (Vanilla Townie) was questioned, and in an accident, slipped on the floor, and slammed his neck into a sharp corner. His carotid artery erupted with blood as the rest of the suspects, frightened by the fountain of red liquid, backed away and waited for an officer to arrive.

EoN5
A Yeohadan officer left the premises of his workplace shortly after midnight. He was on the Night shift, and he'd come out to stretch after sitting in his office for a few hours. A coworker had told him that the place was sometimes dangerous during the late hours, specifically telling him stories of finding dead little girls or meeting hit-and-runs. The officer had laughed it off, but the idea stuck in the back of his mind for quite some time.

After a few months of working the Night shift, however, he found there was nothing much to be afraid of. It was the usual - enter the office, sit at the desk, examine some papers, check up on suspects and criminals. After figuring out that none of his superiors were usually on the Night shift, the officer began to slack off a bit. Sometimes he'd go on walks during Night - there wasn't much to do anyways. Today, he figured, he'd get some late night snack at a nearby open store. He hummed as he walked the streets. It was quiet tonight.

After walking a few blocks, he noticed the store he had in mind. He fished his pocket, getting his wallet ready, and entered in.

***

The officer spent some time chewing his glazed donut, and then, figuring he should get back to work, waved goodbye to the clerk at the counter and exited the store. In one hand, he had a few donuts he decided to take back to the office. He was not supposed to eat in the office, but it wasn't like his superiors were there to see him eat anyways.

He stepped onto the road and began walking back... when he heard a muffled noise from the direction of his workplace. He reasoned it was probably a stray animal out in the dark, but nonetheless, he began to feel nervous. He quieted his steps and quickened his pace, and he prayed that nothing had happened. If something had occurred - if something major had occurred - he'd definitely get fired.

The officer came back to a see a short black figure at the door. He sighed a sigh of relief. It was probably a lost kid.

As he walked closer, however, he froze. He noticed sharp black nails sticking out from the door. He searched his pockets for a flashlight - it was something he usually brought with him, but he couldn't find anything. A sense of fear overtook him as he approached the figure. He gulped as he placed his hand next to the figure's neck... and found no pulse. The kid was dead.

The officer had found the corpse of Fvr_Vain (Vanilla Townie) nailed to the entrance doors of Yeohadan.

EoD6
Only five people were left.
And yet the incompetent Yeohadan officers still had little idea of the culprit.

They'd been busy, yes, and recently, there had been some other important cases, so the best were sent out on that case. This left some lazy officers on the Melly case. They figured it was unsolved for years already, so they'd given up.

Now, with the recent suspect-killing, the upper levels had batted an eye to it, but some of the other officers had become a bit interested. One officer in paricular, although new, seemed eager to solve the case. While his coworkers were out to investigate a fire, he specifically volunteered to stay at Yeohadan and take up the Melly case.

First, he was to check up on the suspects. He looked through their files. It seemed that they were grouped together.
He hesitated...

The past few days he'd heard of some suspects in the same room killing each other. They'd only put them in the same room because other rooms had been quite full. Now, with many cases solved, there was definitely room to allow each suspect of this case their own room. But why wouldn't they?

The upper levels didn't expect to have the suspects kill each other until the last culprit(s) was the only one alive right?

He hurried over to the room. Four people were individually huddled in a corner, and a fifth sat in the middle. It looked like nothing had happened...

He warily opened the door to the room and went in himself, planning to question the suspects to find any new leads on the case. Weirdly enough, once he entered, only three of the five people looked up. They pointed to his nearest corner, where a person sat. The officer, confused, turned to this person, and asked a question.

No response.

He felt something was off, and decided to take asdf334 (Vanilla Townie)'s pulse.
There was none.

He whipped his head around to face the other four people. At this point... didn't they all have to be guilty of murder?

EoN6
Today was an ominous night, with a blood red moon illuminating the sky. Soft wind blew threw the trees, and quieted soon as a figure appeared in the open. Meanwhile, in Yeohadan, the offices were empty and one - no, two less prisoners slept in the room. The corpse of Osmium_ (Vanilla Townie), one innocent citizen, lie quietly in the room, seeming as if it were still alive and just sleeping in a pool of red, and the other prisoner had left the premise, now out in the open.

It was already October 19th. The date he was crazed about. The date that all the children he'd experimented on had been born. He'd been looking for power - the power of a Syniez, akin to the power of Melly to realize anything from one's wishes and dreams. Sadly, they'd almost all failed the experiment, and writhed to death. The ones who survived had escaped... but he'd found him in the prison, before he died.

He laughed, the sound echoing in the Night. He'd tracked the man, and where his corpse was to lay after his death. And tonight, he would go harvest the En Piste from the Syniez, so that he could examine and find what it was he had to do to get himself that power.

And from behind emerged another figure.

"Aha, so it was you, Heathe."

The scientist turned around to see a familiar face - a face he didn't expect to be in Yeohadan.

"What's one of the Tessera's doing here?" He asked, hostile.

The figure smiled wryly. "This was to be a display," he answered, as he walked out under the moonlight. "But you made it much more interesting," he continued, "although we'll have to cut that short now, due to our plans." He drew out his mana, and his opponent reacted in turn.

***

"Did you expect to win against a Tessera?" asked the figure, and he pressed a foot down on the injured scientist.
There was no response. The scientist, it seemed, had gone unconscious. The figure sighed, finding the process of killing tedious now.

"I wonder why the Eftichia Leader insisted on this," sighed he, as he brought a final blow upon sudzoof (Heathe, Serial Killer)'s head.
DO NOT POST UNTIL THIS STATEMENT IS CROSSED OUT.
The game has ended.

- The game is majority hang, meaning that the player (including “No Hang”) with the majority of the votes in the game will be hanged at the very point the majority vote is reached. Thereafter, Twilight will occur, until the End of Day notification is posted. Players may continue to post in Twilight, but no votes will be counted.
- Once the end of day votecount is posted, and a player, if any, is announced dead, the player is officially dead, and Night immediately occurs.
- There is a confirm rule, which players should address as soon as possible. The directions are found somewhere within these rules.
[/hide]
[hide=Laws Regarding Activity]
- Each Day shall be 336 hours long. The moderator may extend Day by any time for whatever reason.
- The end of the Day shall occur every time 336 hours of consecutive Day time has passed.
- Each Night shall be 72 hours long. During Night, players with night actions may perform their night action by posting in their Role PM. Any requests posted during Day will not be processed. At the end of night, players who have any night actions will lose their ability to perform a night action, if they have any. 
- Once the End of Night notice is posted, the next Day will officially start.
- You must show that you understand these edicts and laws by posting in your Role PM the two words I spell with the first letter of the titles of almost every hide tag in this post. If you do not, your votes will not be counted.
- Players must post a non-prod-dodge-post at least once every 24 hours, if not on V/LA. Once the first 24 hours have gone by, if the player has not posted according to this rule, the player will receive an in-game prod. Once the next 24 hours have gone by, the player will receive a PM prod. The player will then accumulate 1 PM prod per 24 hours after the first. After 72 hours, the moderator will replace the player, unless the player posts before the moderator finds a replacement. The moderator will also replace the player if the player has received a total of 5 or more PM prods.
- Request V/LA by posting [b]V/LA: [insert date here] to [insert date here][/b] in the thread or Role PM. As a general rule of thumb, do not post V/LA’s longer than five days.
- Request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in the thread or Role PM.
[/hide]
[hide=Your Behavior] 
- Do not behave inappropriately. This includes language and attitude.
- The player receives a warning for each minor rule infraction. A newbie may receive up to 2 warnings, and an SE/IC up to 1. If a player commits any rule infraction after they have run out of warnings, or if they commit a major rule infraction, they will be modkilled (or force-replaced).
[/hide]

[url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h1988155p14679694]juliankuang(1)[/url][/hide]
[hide=ReadMe.exe]
[hide=EoN0]
Melly Day occurs on February 29th each year. It’s the day that Melly killed Certa, and also the day she killed herself. It’s told in a legend that Melly appears every February 29th looking for Certa…

Charge, an [hide=ordinary citizen]
You are an ordinary citizen [color=#0f0](Vanilla Townie)[/color] in Roserocarta, the world of God. You were not particularly close to the crime scene, but it seems that Yeohadan sees you as a suspect involved in the possible crime.

Your only power, other than your inborn power, is your voice and your vote.
You are Town-Aligned.
[/hide] residing in the Gods’ world, died coincidentally on 2/29/12. Xzhang was discovered dead this date 4 years ago. Their deaths seem similar to the death of Senika 16 years ago, which has been unsolved all these years. Hopefully, the stage that the Creator set up will be able to find the murderer, if any, and avenge the victims’ death.
[/hide]
[hide=EoD1]
On the first day, the citizens noticed that one particular person seemed too obviously suspicious. [color=#f0f]hannahptl (Certa, Jester)[/color] had been lurking around and claiming that she was the one who killed Melly. The citizens, finding the claim believable, hanged her.
[/hide]
[hide=EoN1]
A few hours after the sun set, a few stealthy figures slipped by in the darkness. Other than the rustling noises they accidentally produced while moving, the night was calm and peaceful...

In the wee hours of night, Sena, who had been meeting with Ten, her brother, at Yeohadan to discuss their work on the latest case (and complaining about overtime), stretched and yawned and finally left the organization. She couldn't wait to get a good nights sleep to refresh and refocus on the Melly cases tomorrow.

At the same time, Hiasen had been pondering about Certa, and whether he should have killed him. He tried to reason that Certa would have killed his father, one way or another, if he had not died. Yet Hiasen could not suppress the guilt and shame rising up in his heart - he had thought of Certa as a kind of foster father and friend. He sighed, deciding to rest his mind.

The morning sun rose, its rays of sunlight shining upon the God's world. Suddenly, a startling scream broke the morning silence. Someone had discovered a body in an abandoned laboratory. Upon inspection, Yeohadan police officers concluded that [color=#0f0]fishy15 (Sena, Mason)[/color] was dead. Just as they were about to examine the body, however, they received another notice. The Yeohadan officers covered the eyes of their colleague, and turned to address the new case: another one of the suspects behind the Melly Day cases, [color=#0f0]HannahW (Hiasen, Cop)[/color], was found dead. Her corpse had been found under the shadows of an unnoticing little corner in the streets. It was strange that a child such as him was out so late at night, the Yeohadan officers muttered amongst each other.[/hide]
[hide=EoD2]
Under the custody of the Yeohadan officers, the suspects had nothing much to do. Much of the day passed by silently, although a few brawls broke out. The officers yawned, seeing nothing of interest. As the last police left his shift, however, he noticed that tikkitamatoa was getting strangled by many of the other suspects. He shouted at them, and they froze in silence. 

"What an unruly bunch," the last officer thought. He would have to bring this up with his superiors tomorrow. Hopefully, they'd be able to separate the citizens and criminals under custody so that they wouldn't have to deal with such egregrious behavior.

[b]No one died.[/b]
[/hide]
[hide=EoN2]
It was finally morning. The people of Roserocarta yawned and trickled out of bed, as the sun shone brightly in the sky. The citizens dressed, took their morning breakfast - some going to a nearby cafe. Then, they took to their daily business: whatever they did on the weekends.

An officer groaned. He was supposed to go camping with his family today, but the previous man on duty - a sloth, thought the officer - had had some "emergency" and thus left the shift open. His superiors had determined that a family vacation on a nice Sunday morning was not worth the important duty of guarding a couple of suspects.

He shook his head at this thought, and by chance saw the time on his watch. He glanced up again. The line in front of him was too long. He'd never get the coffee he needed and get to work on time.

"What bad luck," he said, reluctantly exiting the shop. He decided to order his coffee at work. It would be a bit late, and it probably wouldn't be fresh, but it would have to do. Thinking this, the officer sighed as he hustled himself to Yeohadan.

It was a strangely beautiful morning, and the sky was especially clear even in front of the large looming building in front of the officer. He thought of what his wife and children would be doing. His superiors had better give him a day off for today, he thought, as he pushed the door open. He rummaged through a few papers at the front desk, looking for the rooms he was to guard today. It was such boring duty; he'd rather take case notes on the recent villains in the Wanted papers.

He took the keys to the room, and whistled as he walked down the long hallway. He had ordered some coffee from the front desk, and it would arrive in a few minutes.

He thought he smelled something for a second. Perhaps it was coffee? But it couldn't have arrived so soon...
A few steps further he noticed the pungent smell of blood. Alarmed, the officer ran down to the room he was to guard and peered in.

There were two corpses sitting among the fourteen - no, twelve - people in the room. [color=#0f0]NDMath (Vanilla Townie)[/color] and [color=#0f0]tikkitamatoa (Vanilla Townie)[/color] had died through the night... yet the other people in the room were sleeping peacefully. The officer quickly called his superior.

"Are the people I am guarding a group of murderers?" He asked. There was a silence.

"What happened?" His boss questioned from the other side. "They are suspects to a certain case but they haven't been judged yet," the boss added, figuring that the content was appropriate for his employee to know.

"There are two dead people," answered the officer.
[/hide]
[hide=EoD3]
The superiors of the officer ignored when he called. They had suspects, but they were citizens, and surely had rational thought processes. Yet this day proved them wrong. There lie [color=#0f0]kevinmathz (Ran, Miller)[/color] in a pool of blood at the 11 other suspects' feet. They'd killed him, ignoring his claims of innocence throughout the game.

Another one down...

But the police couldn't comprehend. The criminal(s) was narrowing down the list of suspects... which would lead the police to find them. Why would they do so?
[/hide]
[hide=EoN3]
He whisked on his white lab coat, peeked into the dim moonlight, and after seeing no one, he cautiously slid into the shadows. He would report to them what he knew thusfar of the investigation.

Silver light speckled onto the long billowy tails of his coat as he exited into the streets. It was inconvenient to don the white robe - it was an eyecatcher in the darkness of the evening, but he wore it nonetheless, since he knew he was most recognizable doing so. It would ensure that he wasn't wrongly attacked by his allies.

And then, in the streets, he let out a barely audible gasp - he felt a smooth knife rip the side of his coat and slide into his stomach. Immediately, he clutched his front side to stop the blood from leaking.

"Who is it?" [color=#f00]Thornclaw9000 (Psychi, Traitor)[/color] whipped around as he fell towards the cement. The last he saw was an ominous smile hidden behind the tails of his white lab coat... or was it the culprit's?
[/hide]
[hide=EoD4]
They were locked in the dungeon, and had nothing much better to do than point fingers at one another. The sooner the culprit(s) was found, the sooner they would all be released.

But of course, the culprit(s) didn't want to be found.

In the midst of the chaos, the prisoners became restless. Yet, only a day later, they were snoozing as light shone through the small windows in the room. They'd used up all their energy in arguments, important or trivial. 

Bored, one prisoner decided to claim that they were part of the Yeohadan police. The other prisoners stared at him. Why would he be in prison if he were the police? Then, another prisoner woke up to the shouting, and realizing the news, decided to announce the news that he was an actual member of Yeohadan, and that he had never known of any person like the first prisoner who had claimed. The first stared in shock, stating that he had done it merely as a joke, because clearly everyone was very bored.

As the day neared its end, the suspects found their energy draining. They'd gotten tired of the place. There was no way they would find the culprit(s) by just sitting and pointing fingers.

In particular, everyone seemed to suspect one particular person. Some shoved said person against the wall, while others tried to question him, or torture him with whatever tools were available in the room.

After enough of this ensued, a couple of people voiced that it seemed that this one person was innocent - they'd been bruised pretty badly, and seemed to stick to their words.

"Stop!" They called, but it was too late. [color=#0f0]Kbwuf (Vanilla Townie)[/color] slumped down onto the ground, as his spirit finally left his body after undergoing grueling questioning by the mishandling of the other suspects.
[/hide]
[hide=EoN4]
There she sat, stirring in the dark room. Her eyes cautiously swiveled around, and she stood up when she noticed that the figures in the darkness had no signs of moving. She stretched, and looked around, scouting for a path leading outside the room. Or perhaps, she'd have to use his power. She sighed, and slipped out the room, and then out onto the sidewalk in front of the Yeohadan building.

She needed a fresh breath of air after nights staying locked up in the room - nights with those corpses staring up at him. She never thought she would have to dirty her own hands, but it had been necessary.

As she stood outside in the cool night air, she heard footsteps approaching behind her.
It was probably him.

She turned around to face the figure.

"You?" She asked in confusion as she saw someone she didn't expect. As she focused his power in an effort to kill this person, she realized she was a second too late - and soon she saw himself shedding blood on the dark sidewalk. There lie [color=#f00]bigmath (Eftichia Leader, Mafia Goon)[/color] in a pool of murky red ink.

Meanwhile, a certain someone had been strolling around the Mana Association. He hadn't heard of news from Yeohadan for ages, and he was quite worried about Hiasen. He'd tried to obtain info and asked to visit, but the Yeohadan soldiers had been adamant that the official stay out. He could have entered by force, but he'd then enrage Yeohadan, which probably wouldn't benefit Hiasen.

Perhaps he could ask Psychi to help, he thought, and he visited the Mana Association leader's room. He was met with a silent and empty room. Classic.

Just as he sighed and turned to leave, he was met with a face... a familiar one.
"Why are you- " he asked, before he was shot across the room by a blast of power.

He scrambled to collect his thoughts, facing his opponent. [i]Why would that person be here[/i], he wondered. His thoughts were cut short as he was sent propelling against the corner of Psychi's desk. Finally reacting, he tried to grasp the air around him to choke his opponent. However, he himself was weak, and he begin becoming dizzy as he saw the figure approach him, seemingly cool.

[i]Of course, his powers wouldn't have an effect on her, [/i] thought [color=#0f0]chess24 (Kasin, Doctor)[/color] as he left the realm of consciousness.
[/hide]
[hide=EoD5]
The suspects who were alive sat around in a circle, discussing the best plan to find out who was the criminal. They figured the faster they did that, the faster the rest of them would be set free - if they wouldn't get murdered by the criminal. Fed up with the little progress, one particular person decided to claim that had been awake during the Night to identify that another person hadn't moved anywhere. Another person chimed in, naming that someone, to the surprise of all the suspects in the circle. They started to wonder if these two people were in on a plan. 

Despite his confession that he hadn't been awake during the Night, and his cries of innocence, [color=#0f0]BobDBuilder321 (Vanilla Townie)[/color] was questioned, and in an accident, slipped on the floor, and slammed his neck into a sharp corner. His carotid artery erupted with blood as the rest of the suspects, frightened by the fountain of red liquid, backed away and waited for an officer to arrive.
[/hide]
[hide=EoN5]
A Yeohadan officer left the premises of his workplace shortly after midnight. He was on the Night shift, and he'd come out to stretch after sitting in his office for a few hours. A coworker had told him that the place was sometimes dangerous during the late hours, specifically telling him stories of finding dead little girls or meeting hit-and-runs. The officer had laughed it off, but the idea stuck in the back of his mind for quite some time.

After a few months of working the Night shift, however, he found there was nothing much to be afraid of. It was the usual - enter the office, sit at the desk, examine some papers, check up on suspects and criminals. After figuring out that none of his superiors were usually on the Night shift, the officer began to slack off a bit. Sometimes he'd go on walks during Night - there wasn't much to do anyways. Today, he figured, he'd get some late night snack at a nearby open store. He hummed as he walked the streets. It was quiet tonight. 

After walking a few blocks, he noticed the store he had in mind. He fished his pocket, getting his wallet ready, and entered in.

***

The officer spent some time chewing his glazed donut, and then, figuring he should get back to work, waved goodbye to the clerk at the counter and exited the store. In one hand, he had a few donuts he decided to take back to the office. He was not supposed to eat in the office, but it wasn't like his superiors were there to see him eat anyways.

He stepped onto the road and began walking back... when he heard a muffled noise from the direction of his workplace. He reasoned it was probably a stray animal out in the dark, but nonetheless, he began to feel nervous. He quieted his steps and quickened his pace, and he prayed that nothing had happened. If something had occurred - if something major had occurred - he'd definitely get fired.

The officer came back to a see a short black figure at the door. He sighed a sigh of relief. It was probably a lost kid.

As he walked closer, however, he froze. He noticed sharp black nails sticking out from the door. He searched his pockets for a flashlight - it was something he usually brought with him, but he couldn't find anything. A sense of fear overtook him as he approached the figure. He gulped as he placed his hand next to the figure's neck... and found no pulse. The kid was dead.

The officer had found the corpse of [color=#0f0]Fvr_Vain (Vanilla Townie)[/color] nailed to the entrance doors of Yeohadan.
[/hide]
[hide=EoD6]
Only five people were left.
And yet the incompetent Yeohadan officers still had little idea of the culprit.

They'd been busy, yes, and recently, there had been some other important cases, so the best were sent out on that case. This left some lazy officers on the Melly case. They figured it was unsolved for years already, so they'd given up.

Now, with the recent suspect-killing, the upper levels had batted an eye to it, but some of the other officers had become a bit interested. One officer in paricular, although new, seemed eager to solve the case. While his coworkers were out to investigate a fire, he specifically volunteered to stay at Yeohadan and take up the Melly case.

First, he was to check up on the suspects. He looked through their files. It seemed that they were grouped together.
He hesitated...

The past few days he'd heard of some suspects in the same room killing each other. They'd only put them in the same room because other rooms had been quite full. Now, with many cases solved, there was definitely room to allow each suspect of this case their own room. But why wouldn't they?

The upper levels didn't expect to have the suspects kill each other until the last culprit(s) was the only one alive right?

He hurried over to the room. Four people were individually huddled in a corner, and a fifth sat in the middle. It looked like nothing had happened...

He warily opened the door to the room and went in himself, planning to question the suspects to find any new leads on the case. Weirdly enough, once he entered, only three of the five people looked up. They pointed to his nearest corner, where a person sat. The officer, confused, turned to this person, and asked a question.

No response.

He felt something was off, and decided to take [color=#0f0]asdf334 (Vanilla Townie)[/color]'s pulse. 
There was none.

He whipped his head around to face the other four people. At this point... didn't they all have to be guilty of murder?
[/hide]
[hide=EoN6]
Today was an ominous night, with a blood red moon illuminating the sky. Soft wind blew threw the trees, and quieted soon as a figure appeared in the open. Meanwhile, in Yeohadan, the offices were empty and one - no, two less prisoners slept in the room. The corpse of [color=#0f0]Osmium_ (Vanilla Townie)[/color], one innocent citizen, lie quietly in the room, seeming as if it were still alive and just sleeping in a pool of red, and the other prisoner had left the premise, now out in the open.

It was already October 19th. The date he was crazed about. The date that all the children he'd experimented on had been born. He'd been looking for power - the power of a Syniez, akin to the power of Melly to realize anything from one's wishes and dreams. Sadly, they'd almost all failed the experiment, and writhed to death. The ones who survived had escaped... but he'd found him in the prison, before he died.

He laughed, the sound echoing in the Night. He'd tracked the man, and where his corpse was to lay after his death. And tonight, he would go harvest the En Piste from the Syniez, so that he could examine and find what it was he had to do to get himself that power.

And from behind emerged another figure.

"Aha, so it was you, Heathe."

The scientist turned around to see a familiar face - a face he didn't expect to be in Yeohadan.

"What's one of the Tessera's doing here?" He asked, hostile.

The figure smiled wryly. "This was to be a display," he answered, as he walked out under the moonlight. "But you made it much more interesting," he continued, "although we'll have to cut that short now, due to our plans." He drew out his mana, and his opponent reacted in turn.

***

"Did you expect to win against a Tessera?" asked the figure, and he pressed a foot down on the injured scientist.
There was no response. The scientist, it seemed, had gone unconscious. The figure sighed, finding the process of killing tedious now.

"I wonder why the Eftichia Leader insisted on this," sighed he, as he brought a final blow upon [color=#f0f]sudzoof (Heathe, Serial Killer)[/color]'s head.
[/hide]
[/hide]

post_rendered:
Night 1 has now ended a bit early due to personal reasons.
Night Results
A few hours after the sun set, a few stealthy figures slipped by in the darkness. Other than the rustling noises they accidentally produced while moving, the night was calm and peaceful...

In the wee hours of night, Sena, who had been meeting with Ten, her brother, at Yeohadan to discuss their work on the latest case (and complaining about overtime), stretched and yawned and finally left the organization. She couldn't wait to get a good nights sleep to refresh and refocus on the Melly cases tomorrow.

At the same time, Hiasen had been pondering about Certa, and whether he should have killed him. He tried to reason that Certa would have killed his father, one way or another, if he had not died. Yet Hiasen could not suppress the guilt and shame rising up in his heart - he had thought of Certa as a kind of foster father and friend. He sighed, deciding to rest his mind.

The morning sun rose, its rays of sunlight shining upon the God's world. Suddenly, a startling scream broke the morning silence. Someone had discovered a body in an abandoned laboratory. Upon inspection, Yeohadan police officers concluded that fishy15 (Sena, Mason) was dead. Just as they were about to examine the body, however, they received another notice. The Yeohadan officers covered the eyes of their colleague, and turned to address the new case: another one of the suspects behind the Melly Day cases, HannahW (Hiasen, Cop), was found dead. Her corpse had been found under the shadows of an unnoticing little corner in the streets. It was strange that a child such as him was out so late at night, the Yeohadan officers muttered amongst each other.
Day 2 has started, and it will end on 02/20/2020 at 13:00:00 EST.

post_rendered:
Night 2 has now ended (a bit early again).
Night Results
It was finally morning. The people of Roserocarta yawned and trickled out of bed, as the sun shone brightly in the sky. The citizens dressed, took their morning breakfast - some going to a nearby cafe. Then, they took to their daily business: whatever they did on the weekends.

An officer groaned. He was supposed to go camping with his family today, but the previous man on duty - a sloth, thought the officer - had had some "emergency" and thus left the shift open. His superiors had determined that a family vacation on a nice Sunday morning was not worth the important duty of guarding a couple of suspects.

He shook his head at this thought, and by chance saw the time on his watch. He glanced up again. The line in front of him was too long. He'd never get the coffee he needed and get to work on time.

"What bad luck," he said, reluctantly exiting the shop. He decided to order his coffee at work. It would be a bit late, and it probably wouldn't be fresh, but it would have to do. Thinking this, the officer sighed as he hustled himself to Yeohadan.

It was a strangely beautiful morning, and the sky was especially clear even in front of the large looming building in front of the officer. He thought of what his wife and children would be doing. His superiors had better give him a day off for today, he thought, as he pushed the door open. He rummaged through a few papers at the front desk, looking for the rooms he was to guard today. It was such boring duty; he'd rather take case notes on the recent villains in the Wanted papers.

He took the keys to the room, and whistled as he walked down the long hallway. He had ordered some coffee from the front desk, and it would arrive in a few minutes.

He thought he smelled something for a second. Perhaps it was coffee? But it couldn't have arrived so soon...
A few steps further he noticed the pungent smell of blood. Alarmed, the officer ran down to the room he was to guard and peered in.

There were two corpses sitting among the fourteen - no, twelve - people in the room. NDMath (Vanilla Townie) and tikkitamatoa (Vanilla Townie) had died through the night... yet the other people in the room were sleeping peacefully. The officer quickly called his superior.

"Are the people I am guarding a group of murderers?" He asked. There was a silence.

"What happened?" His boss questioned from the other side. "They are suspects to a certain case but they haven't been judged yet," the boss added, figuring that the content was appropriate for his employee to know.

"There are two dead people," answered the officer.
Day 3 has started, and it will end on 03/08/2020 at 13:30:00 EST.

He whisked on his white lab coat, peeked into the dim moonlight, and after seeing no one, he cautiously slid into the shadows. He would report to them what he knew thusfar of the investigation.

Silver light speckled onto the long billowy tails of his coat as he exited into the streets. It was inconvenient to don the white robe - it was an eyecatcher in the darkness of the evening, but he wore it nonetheless, since he knew he was most recognizable doing so. It would ensure that he wasn't wrongly attacked by his allies.

And then, in the streets, he let out a barely audible gasp - he felt a smooth knife rip the side of his coat and slide into his stomach. Immediately, he clutched his front side to stop the blood from leaking.

"Who is it?" Thornclaw9000 (Psychi, Traitor) whipped around as he fell towards the cement. The last he saw was an ominous smile hidden behind the tails of his white lab coat... or was it the culprit's?

post_rendered:
an interesting thing about the traitor role as described by the MS wiki page and by Bob a little while back:
Click to reveal hidden text
because traitor knows other wolves but wolves don't know traitor,
traitor has to simultaneously try to not get hanged AND try to not get killed

what can happen is that since wolves generally know if a traitor exists, the traitor tries to signal his role to the other wolves

there's no guarantee that thorn was doing this, but if he was, then I think his EOD post about his bad feelings about Kbwuf and him telling SK/wolves to not kill him might have been a signal, and the Kbwuf thing was thrown in there to show that he knew Kbwuf was a wolf

but regardless of whether thorn was trying to signal, he started d3 with a hard townread on Kbwuf and at the end voiced suspicions about Kbwuf but never hard pushed him. that's not a towny interaction


anyway I do agree that thorn pushing juliankuang hard is probably a good look for Osmium, though I'm not sure how much towncred I want to give. idk why you're calling thorn's posts wolfy after he's already flipped but whatever

Applying pressure?
I'm going to assume there are two wolves left:

So by what I'm very confident the setup is there are

7 vanilla townies left (including mason)
1 vig
2 scum

Say there is 1 mason left:

Situation 1:

If mason claims, and then scum counterclaims.

What happens: We hang mason or scum. If we hang mason, vig shoots scum. If we hang scum, then scum shoots mason. So, we trade off a VT (mason is pretty much a VT) for a scum, which is optimal.

Situation 2:

If mason claims, then scum doesn't counterclaim. Then we have an innocent child.

What could happen:
- Scum shoots mason, giving vig one night protection which is good.
- Vig shoots somebody. Now we have 6 VT, 1 vig, 2 scum.
- Worse case scenario next day (vig shouldn't shoot) we have 5 VT, 2 scum. Bad.
- Best case scenario, 5 Vt, 1 vig.
- Most likely scenario (by mechanics) 4 VT, 1 vig, 2 scum.

This is complicated my head is spinning.

Anyway, I think our best bet is mason claim tommorow, then possibly a vig claim, but probably not.

They were locked in the dungeon, and had nothing much better to do than point fingers at one another. The sooner the culprit(s) was found, the sooner they would all be released.

But of course, the culprit(s) didn't want to be found.

In the midst of the chaos, the prisoners became restless. Yet, only a day later, they were snoozing as light shone through the small windows in the room. They'd used up all their energy in arguments, important or trivial.

Bored, one prisoner decided to claim that they were part of the Yeohadan police. The other prisoners stared at him. Why would he be in prison if he were the police? Then, another prisoner woke up to the shouting, and realizing the news, decided to announce the news that he was an actual member of Yeohadan, and that he had never known of any person like the first prisoner who had claimed. The first stared in shock, stating that he had done it merely as a joke, because clearly everyone was very bored.

As the day neared its end, the suspects found their energy draining. They'd gotten tired of the place. There was no way they would find the culprit(s) by just sitting and pointing fingers.

In particular, everyone seemed to suspect one particular person. Some shoved said person against the wall, while others tried to question him, or torture him with whatever tools were available in the room.

After enough of this ensued, a couple of people voiced that it seemed that this one person was innocent - they'd been bruised pretty badly, and seemed to stick to their words.

"Stop!" They called, but it was too late. Kbwuf (Vanilla Townie) slumped down onto the ground, as his spirit finally left his body after undergoing grueling questioning by the mishandling of the other suspects.
Night begins now and will end at 16:00:00 EDT on 04/05/2020.

post_rendered:
Night 4 has now ended.
Night Results
There she sat, stirring in the dark room. Her eyes cautiously swiveled around, and she stood up when she noticed that the figures in the darkness had no signs of moving. She stretched, and looked around, scouting for a path leading outside the room. Or perhaps, she'd have to use his power. She sighed, and slipped out the room, and then out onto the sidewalk in front of the Yeohadan building.

She needed a fresh breath of air after nights staying locked up in the room - nights with those corpses staring up at him. She never thought she would have to dirty her own hands, but it had been necessary.

As she stood outside in the cool night air, she heard footsteps approaching behind her.
It was probably him.

She turned around to face the figure.

"You?" She asked in confusion as she saw someone she didn't expect. As she focused his power in an effort to kill this person, she realized she was a second too late - and soon she saw himself shedding blood on the dark sidewalk. There lie bigmath (Eftichia Leader, Mafia Goon) in a pool of murky red ink.

Meanwhile, a certain someone had been strolling around the Mana Association. He hadn't heard of news from Yeohadan for ages, and he was quite worried about Hiasen. He'd tried to obtain info and asked to visit, but the Yeohadan soldiers had been adamant that the official stay out. He could have entered by force, but he'd then enrage Yeohadan, which probably wouldn't benefit Hiasen.

Perhaps he could ask Psychi to help, he thought, and he visited the Mana Association leader's room. He was met with a silent and empty room. Classic.

Just as he sighed and turned to leave, he was met with a face... a familiar one.
"Why are you- " he asked, before he was shot across the room by a blast of power.

He scrambled to collect his thoughts, facing his opponent. Why would that person be here, he wondered. His thoughts were cut short as he was sent propelling against the corner of Psychi's desk. Finally reacting, he tried to grasp the air around him to choke his opponent. However, he himself was weak, and he begin becoming dizzy as he saw the figure approach him, seemingly cool.

Of course, his powers wouldn't have an effect on her, thought chess24 (Kasin, Doctor) as he left the realm of consciousness.
Day 5 has now started, and it will end on 04/19/2020 at 17:01:00 EST.

post_rendered:
@above capitals matter in this game

Here is my thinking.

Two scum:
The scum are probably in the set {asdf334, sudzoof, Osmium_, Fvr_Vain}. If we hang one and vig one, they could be both town (1/6 chance), and then we lose because there's 2 of each team left after the mafia kills someone. They could be one town one scum (2/3 chance), which will result in 3 town vs 1 scum the next day. In this case, we can hang one of the scummy players and vig the other. We win, except for the case that the vig had died and the hang is of a town (1/2 chance to hang town).
In total, assuming the scum identifications are random and the scum are one of those four our chance of losing is 1/12 multiplied by the chance scum kills vig.

One scum:
This is a guaranteed win assuming the scum is one of those four. We just hang one of them and the vig shoots one of them. If they're both town it's 3 vs 1 and we repeat the next day. Even if the vig had died and we hang a town, it's still 2 vs 1 and we hang the final scummy player.

அதனால் நான் கணிதம் செய்து கொண்டிருந்தேன்

Part I:

This will be all about Michael34. Fvr_Vain's posts have no substance and hence my omission of them.

One of the things I had assumed earlier when reading was that thorn's pushing of both Michael34 and bigmath made them both towny. This seemed reasonable considering that thorn had pushed my predecessor and I knew I was town. I still consider the way thorn was acting towards the end of D3 to indicate his paranoia of being killed by mafia and therefore also his reluctance to scumread any mafia let alone push for a mafia hang that day. However, this just isn't the case when thorn pushes either of Michael or bigmath, and if thorn was pushing bigmath, then he could very well have pushed another tone of the mafia. We see this post:
Michael34 wrote:
Thornclaw9000 wrote:
wait a sec since scum shot nd
nd must have been scumreading at least one of them or they're just bad lol
so he scumread sheep/michael/sudz
Vote: Michael34

day 1 of asking mason to claim

How does this logic makes sense? It doesn't make sense in my eyes. Even if I was mafia I would have never killed ND just because he was scumreading me.
This is just a ridiculous jump of logic as there is no reason to single out Michael from the other two players. So I suspect that thorn did this specifically in trying to hint at the fact that he was traitor. However michael's response is not too convincing, and neither does it seem like he sees the traitor connection. Indeed, thorn says later that his "gambit" failed and starts going protective mode with that ridiculous post where he tells people not to shoot him because it will be very bad.

Ok but that is just speculation. So pretend that you didn't read it. I completely failed earlier in realizing that Michael's play is just scummy, having absolutely no content. I was fine with the tone, but I missed the fact that he claimed that he was going to provide his reads but never does. Michael34 proceeds to be most unhelpful. Then there's #728 and #907:
Michael34 wrote:
How many votes are needed for the hang today? I'm down with voting tikki as long as majority of the people agree with that.
Michael34 wrote:
tikkitamatoa wrote:

This is a very villagery post.

I will vote later before the deadline. I want to hear from people about mymathkid.
Michael34 shows that he doesn't have any real opinions on anybody. #728 is quite blatantly riding the wave and in #907, he just reverses opinion out of nowhere just because there's new pressure for a counterwagon. And of course, he proceeds to ask others for their opinion. Michael never has tried to push anyone the entire game. Sometimes he makes suggestions, but inevitably he just goes with the current of town opinion. Then there's #924:
Michael34 wrote:
How do you know that he was shot by the vig? How can you be so sure about it? And why are you so happy that he is killed? He was town no matter what....

Also kbwuf can you also answer these questions?
There's actually no way michael jumped to the conclusion that tikki was town no matter what. He doesn't explain anything about how he got to that conclusion other than by calling a certain post villagery and what's worse is that he asks the question of "Why are you so happy?". Michael doesn't seem to realize that still a lot of people had thought tikki was scum and we know this from how he only responds "Now I get it" after kbwuf having to explain how the fact that tikki was thought to be scum meant there was a vig. Originally I had thought that this was a towny post:
Michael34 wrote:
What's the current votecount?

Also tikki are you a pr? If so then please claim.
This is the only way to save yourself.
But this really isn't. Instead it seems to follow a general trend of Michael asking prs to claim, specifically vig in #548 after N1 and in #1018 after tikki died. Asking for vig to claim can be seen as an excuse to kill a confirmed townie, but I might say #548 was alright. Asking for vig to claim in #1018 is not okay though:
Michael34 wrote:
Can the vig please come out and tell if they killed tikki, so that we actually know the reasons behind nightkills.

But I'm still wondering who would have killed ND.
This is just blatantly unhelpful. "So that we actually know the reasons"? We already know the reason. Asking vig to claim is asking vig to die and its literally middle of the game. If this isn't pr fishing I don't know what is. Anyways, I think those were all the reasons to scumread Michael34 based on his posts alone. Lastly we consider the relation between bigmath and michael. #659:
Michael34 wrote:
I am kinda liking bigmath and kbwuf's posts. I am also townreading bigmath because his analysis is good.

Bigmath if you are scumreading ikarhat then why are you not voting him?

Also mymathkid what is your experience at mafia? Because you seem a beginner to me.

I'm sorry I haven't been posting much lately but my reads will be coming soon.
In the first place bigmath has no analysis, so this is just unwarranted. It's also pretty weird that while townreading bigmath, he chooses to ask them ... whatever that question is. Feel free to think whatever about that. Then there is the posts #756 and #1157 from bigmath that I posted earlier: "And I also have no clue how michael is scummy either." "how is micheal scummy i would only scumread him for inactivity" This correlates with how bigmath reacts whenever he is called scummy or voted, by just being like wait, why?, how? and doesn't really have any defense or things to say. Bigmath changing his mind just 5 posts later isn't helping in #1162 isn't helping:"I don't remember if michael requested replacement or if he is just inactive but if it's the latter then im going to start scumreading him"

So as such, I find it reasonable to believe that Michael was bigmath's scumpartner.

[quote=Michael34][quote=tikkitamatoa][/quote]

Asking for vig to claim can be seen as an excuse to kill a confirmed townie, but I might say #548 was alright. Asking for vig to claim in #1018 is not okay though:

This is just blatantly unhelpful. "So that we actually know the reasons"? We already know the reason. Asking vig to claim is asking vig to die and its literally middle of the game. If this isn't pr fishing I don't know what is. Anyways, I think those were all the reasons to scumread Michael34 based on his posts alone. [quote=Michael34]I am kinda liking bigmath and kbwuf's posts. I am also townreading bigmath because his analysis is good.

"how is micheal scummy i would only scumread him for inactivity" This correlates with how bigmath reacts whenever he is called scummy or voted, by just being like wait, why?, how? and doesn't really have any defense or things to say. Two scum:
The scum are probably in the set {asdf334, sudzoof, Osmium_, Fvr_Vain}. post_rendered:
Night 5 has now ended.
Night Results
A Yeohadan officer left the premises of his workplace shortly after midnight. He was on the Night shift, and he'd come out to stretch after sitting in his office for a few hours. A coworker had told him that the place was sometimes dangerous during the late hours, specifically telling him stories of finding dead little girls or meeting hit-and-runs. The officer had laughed it off, but the idea stuck in the back of his mind for quite some time.

After a few months of working the Night shift, however, he found there was nothing much to be afraid of. It was the usual - enter the office, sit at the desk, examine some papers, check up on suspects and criminals. After figuring out that none of his superiors were usually on the Night shift, the officer began to slack off a bit. Sometimes he'd go on walks during Night - there wasn't much to do anyways. Today, he figured, he'd get some late night snack at a nearby open store. He hummed as he walked the streets. It was quiet tonight.

After walking a few blocks, he noticed the store he had in mind. He fished his pocket, getting his wallet ready, and entered in.

***

The officer spent some time chewing his glazed donut, and then, figuring he should get back to work, waved goodbye to the clerk at the counter and exited the store. In one hand, he had a few donuts he decided to take back to the office. He was not supposed to eat in the office, but it wasn't like his superiors were there to see him eat anyways.

He stepped onto the road and began walking back... when he heard a muffled noise from the direction of his workplace. He reasoned it was probably a stray animal out in the dark, but nonetheless, he began to feel nervous. He quieted his steps and quickened his pace, and he prayed that nothing had happened. If something had occurred - if something major had occurred - he'd definitely get fired.

The officer came back to a see a short black figure at the door. He sighed a sigh of relief. It was probably a lost kid.

As he walked closer, however, he froze. He noticed sharp black nails sticking out from the door. He searched his pockets for a flashlight - it was something he usually brought with him, but he couldn't find anything. A sense of fear overtook him as he approached the figure. He gulped as he placed his hand next to the figure's neck... and found no pulse. The kid was dead.

The officer had found the corpse of Fvr_Vain (Vanilla Townie) nailed to the entrance doors of Yeohadan.
Day 6 has started, and it will end on 05/03/2020 at 22:30:00 EDT.

post_rendered:
Only five people were left.
And yet the incompetent Yeohadan officers still had little idea of the culprit.

They'd been busy, yes, and recently, there had been some other important cases, so the best were sent out on that case. This left some lazy officers on the Melly case. They figured it was unsolved for years already, so they'd given up.

Now, with the recent suspect-killing, the upper levels had batted an eye to it, but some of the other officers had become a bit interested. One officer in paricular, although new, seemed eager to solve the case. While his coworkers were out to investigate a fire, he specifically volunteered to stay at Yeohadan and take up the Melly case.

First, he was to check up on the suspects. He looked through their files. It seemed that they were grouped together.
He hesitated...

The past few days he'd heard of some suspects in the same room killing each other. They'd only put them in the same room because other rooms had been quite full. Now, with many cases solved, there was definitely room to allow each suspect of this case their own room. But why wouldn't they?

The upper levels didn't expect to have the suspects kill each other until the last culprit(s) was the only one alive right?

He hurried over to the room. Four people were individually huddled in a corner, and a fifth sat in the middle. It looked like nothing had happened...

He warily opened the door to the room and went in himself, planning to question the suspects to find any new leads on the case. Weirdly enough, once he entered, only three of the five people looked up. They pointed to his nearest corner, where a person sat. The officer, confused, turned to this person, and asked a question.

No response.

He felt something was off, and decided to take asdf334 (Vanilla Townie)'s pulse.
There was none.

He whipped his head around to face the other four people. At this point... didn't they all have to be guilty of murder?
Night begins now and will end at 13:30:00: EDT on 4/26/2020.

post_rendered:
Night 6 has now ended.
Night Results
Today was an ominous night, with a blood red moon illuminating the sky. Soft wind blew threw the trees, and quieted soon as a figure appeared in the open. Meanwhile, in Yeohadan, the offices were empty and one - no, two less prisoners slept in the room. The corpse of Osmium_ (Vanilla Townie), one innocent citizen, lie quietly in the room, seeming as if it were still alive and just sleeping in a pool of red, and the other prisoner had left the premise, now out in the open.

It was already October 19th. The date he was crazed about. The date that all the children he'd experimented on had been born. He'd been looking for power - the power of a Syniez, akin to the power of Melly to realize anything from one's wishes and dreams. Sadly, they'd almost all failed the experiment, and writhed to death. The ones who survived had escaped... but he'd found him in the prison, before he died.

He laughed, the sound echoing in the Night. He'd tracked the man, and where his corpse was to lay after his death. And tonight, he would go harvest the En Piste from the Syniez, so that he could examine and find what it was he had to do to get himself that power.

And from behind emerged another figure.

"Aha, so it was you, Heathe."

The scientist turned around to see a familiar face - a face he didn't expect to be in Yeohadan.

"What's one of the Tessera's doing here?" He asked, hostile.

The figure smiled wryly. "This was to be a display," he answered, as he walked out under the moonlight. "But you made it much more interesting," he continued, "although we'll have to cut that short now, due to our plans." He drew out his mana, and his opponent reacted in turn.

***

"Did you expect to win against a Tessera?" asked the figure, and he pressed a foot down on the injured scientist.
There was no response. The scientist, it seemed, had gone unconscious. The figure sighed, finding the process of killing tedious now.

"I wonder why the Eftichia Leader insisted on this," sighed he, as he brought a final blow upon sudzoof (Heathe, Serial Killer)'s head.
The game has now ended. Spectators and those who are dead may now post.

post_rendered:
There were a couple of different things about the OP:

1. Voting Rules were a tad more strict, with correct spacing and capitalization needed.
2. Incorrect votecounts became official if 24 hours passed and no one pointed it out - honestly, xzhang spelled some names wrong, and I did too - I did correct them though.
3. Majority hang - most of you might have liked pure deadline or deadline with majority hang backup.
4. The confirm rule was via Role PM, and it might have been worded badly.
5. "Dead" is before "Alive" and could have made things confusing
6. The people in "Dead" are organized by their number, not time of death, and their time of death is not shown under "Dead."
7. The roles of both those dead and those alive are now hidden with hide tags.
8. "Your Significant Actions" shows Votecount and Activity Check links, which may not have been necessary. It does show the time of death, however.
9. Warnings may have been somewhat inconsistent, and I caught them a bit late. There are 30+ deleted posts in this thread.
10. The VT Role PM may have been a bit hard to find with the flavor under ReadMe.exe
post_canonical:
